title: ITSI Adaptive Thresholding Effectiveness, Confidence-Band Calibration, and False-Positive/Negative Drift Detection

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: required — adaptive thresholds, KPI engine, KV collections, Service_KPI_Summary optional

dataSources: index=itsi_summary for KPI alert_value and threshold envelope time series; index=itsi_tracked_alerts for KPI severity transitions and breach density; index=itsi_grouped_alerts for episode disposition and false-positive labels; lookup itsi_kpi_thresholds (KV export) for effective threshold parameters and training age; lookup itsi_threshold_template_inventory.csv for template, owner_team, business_service, runbook_url; index=cmdb sourcetype=itsi_kpi_metadata for deduped KPI metadata joins; optional accelerated datamodel=Service_KPI_Summary for long-baseline drift counters
app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) plus optional Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit for auxiliary drift models
spl:
`comment("UC-13.2.18 ITSI adaptive threshold calibration band_width fp_fn drift PSI multisearch earliest=-7d@d")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@d latest=now is_service_in_maintenance=0
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service_title,service_name,title,"unknown")))
      | eval alert_value=tonumber(coalesce(alert_value,metric_value,0))
      | eval th_lo=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_lower,lower_bound,0))
      | eval th_hi=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_upper,upper_bound,100))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats avg(alert_value) AS hv avg(th_lo) AS tlo avg(th_hi) AS thi dc(_time) AS obs values(service) AS sv BY kpi_id entity_id _time
      | eval service=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(sv),0),"unknown")
      | eval lane="arm_sum" ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | stats count AS tracked_cnt sum(eval(if(match(lower(toString(severity)),"high|critical"),1,0))) AS breach_cnt BY kpi_id entity_id
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_trk" ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | eval fp_hit=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(disposition,comment,""))),"false|benign|noise|duplicate"),1,0)
      | stats count AS ep_cnt sum(fp_hit) AS false_pos_cnt BY kpi_id entity_id
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_grp" ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_kpi_thresholds
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | eval training_window_age_days=tonumber(coalesce(training_age_days,40))
      | eval threshold_lower=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_lower,0))
      | eval threshold_upper=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_upper,100))
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_kv" ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=true count AS tc FROM datamodel=Service_KPI_Summary WHERE nodename=Service_KPI_Summary earliest=-30d@d latest=now BY Service_KPI_Summary.kpiid Service_KPI_Summary.entity_key
      | rename Service_KPI_Summary.kpiid AS kpi_id Service_KPI_Summary.entity_key AS entity_id
      | eval psi_drift=if(tc>8000,0.07,if(tc>800,0.19,0.28))
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_psi" ]
| eval kpi_id=coalesce(kpi_id,"")
| eval entity_id=coalesce(entity_id,"__svc__")
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="arm_trk",tracked_cnt,null()))) AS tracked_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_trk",breach_cnt,null()))) AS breach_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_grp",false_pos_cnt,null()))) AS false_pos_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_grp",ep_cnt,null()))) AS ep_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_kv",training_window_age_days,null()))) AS training_window_age_days max(eval(if(lane=="arm_psi",psi_drift,null()))) AS psi_drift BY kpi_id entity_id
| where lane=="arm_sum"
| sort 0 kpi_id entity_id - _time
| streamstats window=14 current=t global=f stdev(hv) AS roll_std avg(hv) AS roll_avg first(tlo) AS thr_lo first(thi) AS thr_hi BY kpi_id entity_id
| eval mean_value_7d=coalesce(roll_avg,hv,0)
| eval std_7d=coalesce(roll_std,if(mean_value_7d>0.0001,mean_value_7d*0.25,1))
| eval threshold_lower=coalesce(thr_lo,tlo,0)
| eval threshold_upper=coalesce(thr_hi,thi,100)
| eval band_width=if(std_7d>0,(threshold_upper-threshold_lower)/std_7d,999)
| stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(service) AS service latest(mean_value_7d) AS mean_value_7d latest(std_7d) AS std_7d latest(band_width) AS band_width latest(obs) AS sample_count_7d values(threshold_lower) AS tls values(threshold_upper) AS ths max(tracked_cnt) AS tracked_cnt max(breach_cnt) AS breach_cnt max(false_pos_cnt) AS false_pos_cnt max(ep_cnt) AS ep_cnt max(training_window_age_days) AS training_window_age_days max(psi_drift) AS psi_drift BY kpi_id entity_id
| eval threshold_lower=tonumber(coalesce(mvindex(tls,-1),0))
| eval threshold_upper=tonumber(coalesce(mvindex(ths,-1),100))
| eval total_alerts=coalesce(tracked_cnt,0)
| eval fp_rate=if(total_alerts>0,round(100*coalesce(false_pos_cnt,0)/total_alerts,2),0)
| eval total_incidents=coalesce(ep_cnt,0)
| eval fn_num=max(0,total_incidents-coalesce(breach_cnt,0))
| eval fn_rate=if(total_incidents>0,round(100*fn_num/total_incidents,2),0)
| eval data_completeness_pct=if(sample_count_7d<=0,0,round(100*min(sample_count_7d,168)/168,2))
| eventstats median(band_width) AS svc_med_bw BY service
| eval severity=case(
    band_width<1.0 AND fp_rate>30,"critical",
    band_width>8.0 AND fn_rate>25,"critical",
    training_window_age_days>60 AND psi_drift>0.25,"critical",
    band_width>6.0,"high",
    fp_rate>20,"high",
    data_completeness_pct<65,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","halt_template_resync_rebuild_training_window_validate_outlier_quantile_stdev_policies_and_manual_overrides",severity=="high","refine_time_policy_splits_severity_ladder_inheritance_and_entity_level_threshold_drift",severity=="medium","repair_metric_sparsity_extend_kpi_history_and_kv_retention_for_threshold_documents",true(),"maintain_monthly_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness_review_with_oncall_disposition_exports")
| join type=left max=0 kpi_id [| inputlookup itsi_threshold_template_inventory.csv | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(kpi_id)) | fields kpi_id threshold_template owner_team business_service runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 kpi_id [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=itsi_kpi_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(kpi_id)) | dedup kpi_id | fields kpi_id service ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"platform_reliability_engineering")
| eval threshold_template=coalesce(threshold_template,"unassigned_template")
| eval service=coalesce(service,business_service,"unspecified_service")
| table _time service kpi_id owner_team threshold_template band_width fp_rate fn_rate training_window_age_days data_completeness_pct sample_count_7d severity recommended_action
description: Detects mis-calibrated Splunk ITSI adaptive thresholds by fusing KPI value and envelope series from itsi_summary with tracked-alert breach rates, grouped-alert disposition proxies, KV-backed threshold parameters, optional Service_KPI_Summary baselines, and governance lookups so teams see band_width anomalies, false-positive and false-negative drift, stale training windows, and template inheritance breakage before customer impact.
value: Reliability leaders defend signal-to-noise budgets by proving when adaptive bands are too tight or too loose, when training data no longer matches live behavior, and when template propagation fights manual overrides. Episode managers gain defensible links between analyst dispositions and statistical calibration, while finance and risk stakeholders see evidence that paging spend tracks genuine incident pressure rather than threshold accidents.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the comfort band on a thermostat that learned the house only during a cold month, then summer arrives and the system thinks huge swings are fine until people are miserable. We measure whether those automatic comfort ranges are too loose, too tight, drifting sideways, or fighting a calendar nobody updated, so teams fix the tuning before real trouble hides inside quiet charts.
implementation: Forward itsi_summary, itsi_tracked_alerts, and itsi_grouped_alerts with stable kpi_id and entity_id keys; export itsi_kpi_thresholds and maintain itsi_threshold_template_inventory.csv under configuration management; land cmdb sourcetype=itsi_kpi_metadata; optionally accelerate Service_KPI_Summary; schedule uc_13_2_18_itsi_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness with earliest=-7d@d; route critical and high rows to platform reliability with Service Analyzer and threshold editor drilldowns; archive weekly CSV exports for governance.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with service reliability engineers who curate Splunk IT Service Intelligence KPI policies, the ML operations team that governs adaptive retraining cadence, and episode managers who record analyst dispositions on grouped alerts. This document is deliberately not UC-13.2.1 service health regression, not UC-13.2.2 raw KPI degradation burn rate, not UC-13.2.3 episode mean time to repair, and not UC-13.2.6 Event Analytics rules engine throughput. It focuses on whether adaptive confidence bands are calibrated: too narrow yields alert storms and noisy paging, too wide hides genuine customer-impacting excursions, and training-window drift disconnects live traffic from the distribution the model memorized.

Adaptive threshold families in ITSI include static numeric cutoffs, range envelopes, standard-deviation multipliers around a moving mean, quantile tails estimated from historical buckets, and density-style policies that treat the empirical distribution as the ground truth. Each family trades sensitivity against noise differently. Standard deviation policies assume roughly unimodal, stationary variance; they explode when a KPI is bimodal because morning batch and daytime interactive traffic share one statistic. Quantile policies honor heavy tails but can lag abrupt scale changes when the training window is long. Density thresholds need sufficient sample mass; sparse KPIs collapse to degenerate bands. Range policies are honest when physics dictates hard limits but cruel when seasonality shifts the entire operating point.

Training data windows and retrain cadence are first-class risk surfaces. A window that straddles a major outage teaches the model that catastrophe is normal variance, widening bands until real incidents look like whispers. A window that is too short chases noise and narrows bands until every Tuesday traffic bump pages on-call. Time-policy splits separate peak, off-peak, weekend, and holiday calendars so a single template does not average incompatible behaviors. Misaligned boundaries around daylight saving shifts can park legitimate load into the wrong policy slice until someone notices the clock moved.

KPI history retention and Key Value store footprint matter because adaptive documents, threshold templates, and per-entity overrides persist in collections and configuration objects. Aggressive retention trims cost but erases the long tail needed for quantile stability. Bloated retention without compaction raises KV merge pressure and slows template propagation, which looks like mysterious threshold lag in the UI while searches still succeed.

Severity-tier inheritance maps info, low, medium, high, and critical ladders from templates onto services and entities. When inheritance breaks, entities can inherit a template meant for batch jobs while running interactive latency KPIs, producing band_width anomalies that dwarf sibling entities. Manual overrides during incidents save the day once, then get overwritten by the next template sync unless governance marks sticky overrides.

Operational quality scorecards should track sample_count_7d, gap percentage, duplicate entity churn, and whether base searches skip. A KPI that reports zero for half the week trains adaptive engines on a spike-to-zero pattern that looks stable yet yields useless bands. Aggregation changes, such as swapping average for ninety-fifth percentile mid-flight without a training reset, look like drift in every statistical test.

Ground truth for false positives and false negatives rarely lives in one index. Tracked alerts show what crossed a threshold. Grouped alerts and episode closure codes carry analyst labels such as benign, duplicate, or true incident. Change tickets and external incident systems provide independent incident counts for missed-detection math. This use case assumes you export disposition fields into itsi_grouped_alerts or mirror them into lookup columns keyed by kpi_id and entity_id.

Role-based access for reading itsi_summary, itsi_tracked_alerts, itsi_grouped_alerts, cmdb mirrors, and KV-backed lookups requires least-privilege automation accounts. Never embed personal admin tokens in scheduled searches. Legal reviewers may require masking customer identifiers in service titles; keep business_service in governance CSVs instead of free-text logs when possible.

Risk briefing: adaptive threshold tuning is politically sensitive because it mixes statistics with on-call pain. Pair this alert with change metadata so planned training-window resets do not page executives. Executive stakeholders care about customer journeys, not standard deviations; translate band_width outliers into plain-language risk statements in weekly reviews.

Differentiation recap: threshold-band width normalized by recent dispersion, false-positive rate from analyst disposition, false-negative proxy from missed crossings versus incident totals, training-window age, population stability style drift score from summarized history, data completeness, template inheritance context, and routed remediation tokens. It is not generic service health scoring, not correlation engine saturation by itself, and not episode lifecycle analytics in isolation.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane itsi_summary must retain hourly or finer KPI evaluation rows with alert_value analogs, threshold_lower and threshold_upper when emitted, kpi_id, entity_id or entity_key, service_title fields, and maintenance flags such as is_service_in_maintenance so change windows do not train catastrophe distributions. Validate sourcetype and index routing after upgrades; ITSI minor versions sometimes rename fields.

Lane itsi_tracked_alerts carries KPI severity transitions and correlation metadata. Normalize kpi_id and entity_id identically to itsi_summary. Retain severity text that distinguishes high and critical crossings used in breach_cnt logic.

Lane itsi_grouped_alerts carries episode outcomes. Map analyst disposition, closure comment, or status tokens into fp_hit logic using conservative case-insensitive patterns for false, benign, noise, and duplicate. If your deployment stores disposition only in an external tool, schedule a low-frequency export into cmdb sourcetype=itsi_episode_dispositions and join on episode_id in a follow-on UC; this gold search assumes at least proxy fields exist on grouped alerts.

Maintain lookup itsi_kpi_thresholds exported from KV or REST inventory jobs with columns for kpi_id, entity_id, training_window_age_days, threshold_lower, threshold_upper, threshold_template identifiers, and policy type flags. Refresh after every template publish. Document whether rows represent effective thresholds after inheritance or template-only defaults.

Maintain lookup itsi_threshold_template_inventory.csv with kpi_id when templates are KPI-specific, or service_id bridges when templates attach at service level; include threshold_template, owner_team, business_service, and runbook_url for pager narratives. Configuration management should own the file; stale ownership routes fixes to the wrong queue.

Maintain cmdb index sourcetype=itsi_kpi_metadata with deduped kpi_id rows for service aliases, business_unit, data owner, and optional golden_signal flags. This UC joins that feed for service naming consistency when itsi_summary titles drift.

Optional acceleration: accelerate Service_KPI_Summary data model summaries when your estate already models KPI history for glass tables. The tstats arm is optional; if the data model is absent, replace the arm with a summary-index query that replays historical alert_value distributions for PSI-style drift estimation.

props.conf and transforms.conf should preserve _time fidelity on itsi indexes and flatten nested JSON at ingest if Episode Review emits structured blobs. Clock skew between search heads and indexers distorts streamstats windows; monitor NTP elsewhere using platform hygiene UCs.

Volume controls: multisearch fans five arms every run. Materialize hourly rollups per kpi_id and entity_id into a summary index when Job Inspector shows queue time above internal objectives, then point operational alerts at summaries while retaining this full search for investigations.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_18_itsi_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness with a fifteen-minute to hourly schedule depending on cardinality, dispatch earliest=-7d@d, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after organizational tuning. Throttle duplicate kpi_id and entity_id pairs for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include runbook_url in custom alert actions when the field is present.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch pulls KPI series and threshold envelopes from itsi_summary, notable density from itsi_tracked_alerts, episode disposition proxies from itsi_grouped_alerts, effective threshold parameters from itsi_kpi_thresholds, and long-baseline counts from Service_KPI_Summary via tstats to approximate drift. eventstats stitches cross-lane counters onto each hourly KPI row before streamstats computes fourteen-point rolling dispersion per kpi_id and entity_id. stats collapses to one row per kpi_id and entity_id with band_width, fp_rate, and fn_rate. eventstats adds service-level median band context. case maps severity tiers. join enriches owner_team and template metadata from CSV and cmdb.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.18 ITSI adaptive threshold calibration band_width fp_fn drift PSI multisearch earliest=-7d@d")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@d latest=now is_service_in_maintenance=0
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service_title,service_name,title,"unknown")))
      | eval alert_value=tonumber(coalesce(alert_value,metric_value,0))
      | eval th_lo=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_lower,lower_bound,0))
      | eval th_hi=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_upper,upper_bound,100))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats avg(alert_value) AS hv avg(th_lo) AS tlo avg(th_hi) AS thi dc(_time) AS obs values(service) AS sv BY kpi_id entity_id _time
      | eval service=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(sv),0),"unknown")
      | eval lane="arm_sum" ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | stats count AS tracked_cnt sum(eval(if(match(lower(toString(severity)),"high|critical"),1,0))) AS breach_cnt BY kpi_id entity_id
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_trk" ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | eval fp_hit=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(disposition,comment,""))),"false|benign|noise|duplicate"),1,0)
      | stats count AS ep_cnt sum(fp_hit) AS false_pos_cnt BY kpi_id entity_id
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_grp" ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_kpi_thresholds
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"")))
      | eval entity_id=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_id,entity_key,"__svc__")))
      | eval training_window_age_days=tonumber(coalesce(training_age_days,40))
      | eval threshold_lower=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_lower,0))
      | eval threshold_upper=tonumber(coalesce(threshold_upper,100))
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_kv" ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=true count AS tc FROM datamodel=Service_KPI_Summary WHERE nodename=Service_KPI_Summary earliest=-30d@d latest=now BY Service_KPI_Summary.kpiid Service_KPI_Summary.entity_key
      | rename Service_KPI_Summary.kpiid AS kpi_id Service_KPI_Summary.entity_key AS entity_id
      | eval psi_drift=if(tc>8000,0.07,if(tc>800,0.19,0.28))
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="arm_psi" ]
| eval kpi_id=coalesce(kpi_id,"")
| eval entity_id=coalesce(entity_id,"__svc__")
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="arm_trk",tracked_cnt,null()))) AS tracked_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_trk",breach_cnt,null()))) AS breach_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_grp",false_pos_cnt,null()))) AS false_pos_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_grp",ep_cnt,null()))) AS ep_cnt max(eval(if(lane=="arm_kv",training_window_age_days,null()))) AS training_window_age_days max(eval(if(lane=="arm_psi",psi_drift,null()))) AS psi_drift BY kpi_id entity_id
| where lane=="arm_sum"
| sort 0 kpi_id entity_id - _time
| streamstats window=14 current=t global=f stdev(hv) AS roll_std avg(hv) AS roll_avg first(tlo) AS thr_lo first(thi) AS thr_hi BY kpi_id entity_id
| eval mean_value_7d=coalesce(roll_avg,hv,0)
| eval std_7d=coalesce(roll_std,if(mean_value_7d>0.0001,mean_value_7d*0.25,1))
| eval threshold_lower=coalesce(thr_lo,tlo,0)
| eval threshold_upper=coalesce(thr_hi,thi,100)
| eval band_width=if(std_7d>0,(threshold_upper-threshold_lower)/std_7d,999)
| stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(service) AS service latest(mean_value_7d) AS mean_value_7d latest(std_7d) AS std_7d latest(band_width) AS band_width latest(obs) AS sample_count_7d values(threshold_lower) AS tls values(threshold_upper) AS ths max(tracked_cnt) AS tracked_cnt max(breach_cnt) AS breach_cnt max(false_pos_cnt) AS false_pos_cnt max(ep_cnt) AS ep_cnt max(training_window_age_days) AS training_window_age_days max(psi_drift) AS psi_drift BY kpi_id entity_id
| eval threshold_lower=tonumber(coalesce(mvindex(tls,-1),0))
| eval threshold_upper=tonumber(coalesce(mvindex(ths,-1),100))
| eval total_alerts=coalesce(tracked_cnt,0)
| eval fp_rate=if(total_alerts>0,round(100*coalesce(false_pos_cnt,0)/total_alerts,2),0)
| eval total_incidents=coalesce(ep_cnt,0)
| eval fn_num=max(0,total_incidents-coalesce(breach_cnt,0))
| eval fn_rate=if(total_incidents>0,round(100*fn_num/total_incidents,2),0)
| eval data_completeness_pct=if(sample_count_7d<=0,0,round(100*min(sample_count_7d,168)/168,2))
| eventstats median(band_width) AS svc_med_bw BY service
| eval severity=case(
    band_width<1.0 AND fp_rate>30,"critical",
    band_width>8.0 AND fn_rate>25,"critical",
    training_window_age_days>60 AND psi_drift>0.25,"critical",
    band_width>6.0,"high",
    fp_rate>20,"high",
    data_completeness_pct<65,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","halt_template_resync_rebuild_training_window_validate_outlier_quantile_stdev_policies_and_manual_overrides",severity=="high","refine_time_policy_splits_severity_ladder_inheritance_and_entity_level_threshold_drift",severity=="medium","repair_metric_sparsity_extend_kpi_history_and_kv_retention_for_threshold_documents",true(),"maintain_monthly_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness_review_with_oncall_disposition_exports")
| join type=left max=0 kpi_id [| inputlookup itsi_threshold_template_inventory.csv | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(kpi_id)) | fields kpi_id threshold_template owner_team business_service runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 kpi_id [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=itsi_kpi_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(kpi_id)) | dedup kpi_id | fields kpi_id service ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"platform_reliability_engineering")
| eval threshold_template=coalesce(threshold_template,"unassigned_template")
| eval service=coalesce(service,business_service,"unspecified_service")
| table _time service kpi_id owner_team threshold_template band_width fp_rate fn_rate training_window_age_days data_completeness_pct sample_count_7d severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_2_18_itsi_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_18_itsi_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: when cardinality explodes, pre-aggregate itsi_summary to one-hour means in a summary index keyed on kpi_id and entity_id, shrink the multisearch window to earliest=-48h@h for alerts, and keep seven-day replay searches ad hoc.

Step 4 — Validate

Begin in a lab service with synthetic KPI traffic. Induce a narrow-band policy by tightening standard deviation multipliers, confirm band_width falls and fp_rate rises in Splunk within two hours, then restore policy and confirm return toward baseline.

Induce a wide-band policy by lengthening training windows after injecting an outage slice; confirm band_width rises and fn_rate climbs when ground-truth incidents fail to breach.

Validate disposition joins by closing a test episode as benign; confirm fp_rate responds when false_pos_cnt increments.

Validate itsi_kpi_thresholds freshness by altering training_window_age_days in the lookup export; confirm training_window_age_days and severity react when paired with elevated psi_drift from the tstats arm.

Cross-check cmdb metadata join by running the subsearch alone; confirm dedup kpi_id returns one row and service aliases match Service Analyzer titles.

Negative validation: after recovery, when band_width sits between two and six, fp_rate is below ten percent, fn_rate is below ten percent, and data_completeness_pct is above eighty, filtering to critical and high should yield zero rows for canary KPIs.

Synthetic drift: temporarily point a canary KPI at a backup index with a different scale; confirm psi_drift and severity escalate until you reset training data or split time policies.

Runbook linkage: attach validation screenshots to the configuration management ticket whenever template inheritance changes.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Bands shrink dramatically after an upgrade toggles aggressive outlier rejection: pause auto propagation, compare pre-upgrade and post-upgrade training populations, restore conservative outlier settings, and retrain on a clean window with change metadata attached.

Case 2 — Training window straddles a prolonged outage so quantile tails absorb the failure as normal: exclude the outage interval in the training editor, shorten the window temporarily, and rebuild templates before re-enabling adaptive mode.

Case 3 — Entity churn from discovery adds a volatile storefront pod entity that inherits a latency template meant for stable APIs: split templates by entity rules, move volatile entities to a dedicated template with wider quantile tails, and document inheritance exceptions.

Case 4 — Sparse KPI data yields constant-zero alert_value hours then spikes; adaptive bands hug zero until spikes look like novelty: fix upstream ingestion gaps first, then fall back to static range policies until sample_count_7d exceeds governance minimums.

Case 5 — Quantile policy focuses on the upper tail and misses rapid drops in success rate: add mirrored lower-tail quantile policies or pair with range floors on complementary KPIs, then replay false calm incidents in a sandbox.

Case 6 — Standard deviation policy wobbles on bimodal batch versus interactive traffic: switch to time-policy splits plus quantile thresholds per slice, or separate KPIs rather than forcing one distribution.

Case 7 — On-call edits an entity threshold but the next template sync overwrites it: mark manual overrides in the CMDB row, create a sticky exception workflow, or move the entity to a non-inherited policy until the template catches up.

Case 8 — Daylight saving shifts boundary times so peak traffic lands in off-peak policies: audit time_policy timezone fields, rerun calendar validation, and adjust split minutes with executive sign-off.

Case 9 — KPI feed swaps sourcetype and changes numeric scale without a training reset: declare a data-source change ticket, reset adaptive training, and compare PSI drift before and after backfill.

Case 10 — Aggregation method changes from average to ninety-fifth percentile mid-quarter without communication: revert or freeze training until historical series is recomputed, then publish a new template version with explicit aggregation labels.

Case 11 — Training data source decommissioning forces a silent fallback to static thresholds with stale numbers: restore historical summaries from archive, rebuild KV exports, and validate itsi_kpi_thresholds rows before closing the decommission ticket.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists thirteen columns including owner_team and threshold_template.
visualization: Threshold effectiveness dashboard with severity-colored table by kpi_id, sparklines for band_width and fp_rate, training age gauges, template inheritance tree drilldowns, and disposition histograms from grouped alerts.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Alerts.severity) AS alert_sev count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.signature
| join type=left max=0 Alerts.signature [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host ]
| table Alerts.signature alert_sev cpu_load
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Alerts summaries when ITSI notables map cleanly into Alerts.signature for cross-domain disposition analytics; accelerate Performance summaries for hosts backing KPI entities when correlating infra pressure to threshold crossings; accelerate Service_KPI_Summary when the data model is already in use for glass tables.

knownFalsePositives: Planned training-window resets after major architecture migrations temporarily inflate psi_drift and fp_rate until the new distribution stabilizes; require change tickets before paging service owners. Intentional manual overrides during major incidents can make template inheritance appear broken until sync windows complete; annotate override tickets in the CMDB feed. KPI source data backfill after indexer lag replays history and can skew short-window dispersion metrics; gate alerts during documented backfill intervals. Holiday calendar excluded periods legitimately remove traffic slices and can narrow bands for seasonally quiet KPIs; compare against holiday metadata before declaring mis-calibration. Seasonality boundaries at fiscal quarter close produce abrupt but expected level shifts; downgrade severity when finance calendars explain the jump. Content-pack template imports may flash high fp_rate for an hour while entities remap; validate inventory freshness. Canary KPIs with artificially low cardinality trigger brittle quantile estimates; exclude them from executive rollups. Duplicate entity_id labels from discovery bugs merge unrelated traffic; fix entity rules before trusting fn_rate. Service_KPI_Summary acceleration gaps can zero the tstats arm and drop psi_drift to defaults; treat missing acceleration as a deployment gap, not a threshold failure.
hardware: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads running ITSI with indexer clusters retaining itsi_summary, itsi_tracked_alerts, and itsi_grouped_alerts, typically Linux or Kubernetes-hosted Splunk containers with KV store capacity for threshold documents and optional MLTK compute roles for auxiliary drift scoring.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.2.1 Service Health Score regression detection, UC-13.2.2 per-KPI degradation burn-rate alerting, UC-13.2.3 episode MTTR analytics, UC-13.2.6 Event Analytics rules engine health, standalone Enterprise Security use cases, or greenfield ITSI installation procedures without existing KPI telemetry.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab ITSI tenant, narrow adaptive bands on a canary KPI using aggressive standard deviation multipliers, generate tracked alerts and grouped-alert benign dispositions, confirm uc_13_2_18_itsi_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness returns severity high or critical within two hours with non-null band_width, fp_rate, and recommended_action for the canary kpi_id.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring conservative policies and clearing synthetic dispositions, when band_width stays between two and six, fp_rate remains below ten percent, fn_rate below ten percent, and data_completeness_pct above eighty for eight consecutive runs, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for the canary kpi_id.

evidence: Saved search uc_13_2_18_itsi_adaptive_threshold_effectiveness with scheduled runs, exported CSV rows joined to itsi_threshold_template_inventory.csv, and ticketed screenshots from KPI threshold editors and Episode Review for the same kpi_id.
